summary:
Appeal from the Circuit Court of the United States for the Eastern District of Michigan.

The case is stated in the opinion of the court.
headnotes: A., the owner of lands, covenanted that by making certain payments within a period named B. might become equally interested in them. B. did not agree to purchase, and he never made any payment. Held, that an estate in the lands was not by the contract vested in B., and that his failure to make payment within the time limited therefor worked a forfeiture of his privilege under the contract.
attorneys: Mr. D. C. Holbrook and Mr. H. H. Wells for the appellant., Mr. Alfred Russell and Mr. Henry M. Campbell for the appellee.
